hearings.remarks:
The matter was called for hearing before the Hon’ble Court at 10:30 a.m. The parties were present through their respective counsels. The Court took note of the appearances and directed that the proceedings be recorded accordingly.

The counsel for the petitioner opened the submissions. It was stated that the petitioner had approached the Court seeking redressal of certain grievances arising out of administrative and procedural lapses. The counsel submitted that the petitioner’s legal rights had been affected due to non-compliance with mandatory requirements. Reference was made to documentary evidence already placed on record, including affidavits and supporting documents. The counsel emphasized that the petitioner was compelled to seek judicial intervention after repeated representations to the authorities went unanswered.

The Court then allowed the counsel to present detailed arguments. The submissions primarily revolved around the principle of natural justice and the need for fair treatment. The counsel argued that technical objections raised by the respondent could not override the fundamental rights of the petitioner. It was further urged that the Court should adopt a substantive approach rather than a mechanical reliance on procedure.

Thereafter, the counsel for the respondent presented their arguments. It was contended that the petitioner’s claims were not sustainable in law. The respondent placed reliance on statutory provisions and previous judgments in support of their case. The counsel submitted that the petitioner had failed to comply with prescribed timelines and therefore could not claim relief. It was further argued that the respondent had acted within the bounds of law and no violation of rights had taken place.

The Court listened to the submissions from both sides and posed certain questions to the counsels. Clarifications were sought regarding the timeline of events, the admissibility of certain documents, and the specific statutory provisions being relied upon. Both counsels responded to the queries raised by the Court and placed additional clarifications on record.

During the course of the hearing, the Court observed that the matter involved issues of both procedure and substantive rights. The Court noted the importance of balancing technical compliance with the overarching need for justice. The counsels were advised to submit brief written notes of arguments, along with a list of authorities cited, before the next date of hearing.

The Court further directed that the documents filed by both parties be properly indexed and numbered in order to avoid inconvenience in future hearings. The registry was instructed to ensure that all records were complete and accessible to the Court.

After hearing the counsels and considering the submissions made, the Court ordered that the matter be listed for further hearing on the next date. The counsels were directed to be prepared with their final submissions. The Court clarified that no unnecessary adjournments would be granted.

The proceedings concluded at 1:15 p.m.

hearings.remarks: The hearign was doen in order od the district court to be maintained properly

Respected Sir/Madam, the present matter is listed for hearing today. The applicant submits that all relevant documents have already been placed on record in accordance with law. The issues involved are purely legal in nature and require adjudication on merits. No prejudice will be caused to the opposite party if the reliefs prayed for are considered at this stage. The applicant is ready to assist this Hon’ble Court on facts as well as law. In the interest of justice, equity, and fair play, it is humbly prayed that this Hon’ble Court may kindly consider the submissions and pass appropriate orders as deemed fit and proper in the circumstances of the case.

Respected Sir/Madam,

The present matter is listed for hearing today and is ripe for consideration by this Hon’ble Court.

All relevant documents and pleadings have already been duly filed and form part of the record.

The issues involved are primarily legal in nature and do not require further factual inquiry.

No prejudice will be caused to the opposite party if the matter is heard on merits at this stage.

The applicant is fully prepared to assist this Hon’ble Court on both facts and law.

It is therefore humbly prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to consider the submissions and pass appropriate orders in the interest of justice.
hearings.remarks_plain_text:
Respected Sir/Madam,

The present matter is listed for hearing today and is ripe for consideration by this Hon’ble Court.

All relevant documents and pleadings have already been duly filed and form part of the record.

The issues involved are primarily legal in nature and do not require further factual inquiry.

No prejudice will be caused to the opposite party if the matter is heard on merits at this stage.

The applicant is fully prepared to assist this Hon’ble Court on both facts and law.

It is therefore humbly prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to consider the submissions and pass appropriate orders in the interest of justice.

hearings.remarks: Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned counsel for the respondent. Record perused. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner has a prima facie case and that the balance of convenience lies in his favour. It is further submitted that in the absence of protection, the petitioner is likely to suffer irreparable loss and injury. Per contra, learned counsel for the respondent opposes the prayer and submits that no grounds are made out for grant of any relief at this stage. It is contended that the application is premature and liable to be dismissed. Having considered the rival submissions and the material placed on record, this Court is of the view that the issues raised require consideration. Without expressing any opinion on the merits of the case, and in the interest of justice, limited protection is granted. Accordingly, parties are directed to maintain status quo with respect to the subject matter of the dispute till the next date of hearing. List the matter on the next date for further proceedings.
hearings.remarks_plain_text:
Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned counsel for the respondent. Record perused.
Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner has a prima facie case and that the balance of convenience lies in his favour. It is further submitted that in the absence of protection, the petitioner is likely to suffer irreparable loss and injury.
Per contra, learned counsel for the respondent opposes the prayer and submits that no grounds are made out for grant of any relief at this stage. It is contended that the application is premature and liable to be dismissed.
Having considered the rival submissions and the material placed on record, this Court is of the view that the issues raised require consideration. Without expressing any opinion on the merits of the case, and in the interest of justice, limited protection is granted.
Accordingly, parties are directed to maintain status quo with respect to the subject matter of the dispute till the next date of hearing.
List the matter on the next date for further proceedings.

hearings.remarks: A URT (University Result Tribunal / Unfair Means Review Tribunal) hearing order is a formal written decision issued after examining a student’s grievance related to examination results, unfair means allegations, or evaluation disputes. The order records that the tribunal duly considered the student’s representation, supporting documents, examination records, and the report of the concerned examination authorities. It briefly notes the date of hearing, the presence or absence of the student and officials, and the opportunity given to the student to present their case in line with principles of natural justice. After careful evaluation of facts and evidence, the tribunal arrives at a reasoned conclusion, clearly stating whether the allegations are upheld, modified, or set aside. If relief is granted, the order specifies the exact nature of relief, such as correction of marks, declaration of result, reduction or removal of penalty, or permission to reappear in the examination. If the appeal is rejected, the order mentions the grounds for rejection and confirms the earlier decision of the university or examination body. The URT hearing order is binding on the concerned academic authorities and must be implemented within the stipulated time frame. It serves as an official record ensuring transparency, fairness, and accountability in the examination and evaluation process.
